amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: E. coli transitions from aerobic to anaerobic metabolism through two major regulatory systems: FNR (Fumarate and Nitrate Reduction regulator) and ArcAB (Aerobic Respiration Control). FNR is an oxygen-sensitive transcription factor containing a [4Fe-4S] cluster that is active under anaerobiosis, directly activating genes for anaerobic respiration (nitrate, DMSO, fumarate reductases) and repressing aerobic genes. ArcAB is a two-component system where the sensor kinase ArcB detects reduced quinones in the membrane under low oxygen, phosphorylating the response regulator ArcA which then represses genes for aerobic metabolism (TCA cycle enzymes, cytochrome oxidases) and activates anaerobic genes. Together these systems coordinate the metabolic shift from oxidative phosphorylation to fermentation and anaerobic respiration. This flowchart illustrates oxygen sensing mechanisms, signal transduction, and the coordinated transcriptional reprogramming.

    A4 --> A5[FNR pathway activation]

    A10 --> A12[/Cluster degraded to (2Fe-2S) and apo-FNR/]

    A11 --> A14["\"FNR with intact (4Fe-4S)2+ cluster\""]

    A16 --> A17[FNR activates anaerobic genes]

    A18 -->|narGHJI| A19[Nitrate reductase - NO3- to NO2-]
    A18 -->|dmsABC| A20[DMSO reductase - DMSO to DMS]
    A18 -->|frdABCD| A21[Fumarate reductase - fumarate to succinate]
    A18 -->|nirB| A22[Nitrite reductase - NO2- to NH4+]
    A18 -->|adhE| A23[Alcohol dehydrogenase - fermentation]

    A19 --> A25[Anaerobic respiration with nitrate]
    A20 --> A26[Anaerobic respiration with DMSO]
    A21 --> A27[Anaerobic respiration with fumarate]
    A22 --> A25
    A23 --> A28[Fermentation - mixed acid]
    A24 --> A28
    
    A16 --> A29[/FNR represses aerobic genes/]

    A30 -->|nuoA-N| A32[NADH dehydrogenase I - proton pump]
    A30 -->|sdhCDAB| A33[Succinate dehydrogenase - TCA cycle]
    
    A31 --> A34[Reduced aerobic respiration]
    A32 --> A34
    A33 --> A34
    
    A6 --> A35[ArcB sensor kinase in membrane]

    A37 -->|Reduced quinones - anaerobic| A39["\"Low O2: quinones accumulate in reduced form\""]
    
    A38 --> A40[ArcB remains unphosphorylated]
    A40 --> A41[No signal transduction]
    
    A39 --> A42[Reduced quinones interact with ArcB]
    A42 --> A43[ArcB autophosphorylation activated]
    A43 --> A44[His292 on ArcB phosphorylated]
    
    A44 --> A45[Phosphotransfer to Asp576 on ArcB]

    A46 --> A47{ArcA available?}
    A47 -->|Yes| A48[Phosphotransfer to Asp54 on ArcA]
    A47 -->|No| Wait1[Wait for ArcA]
    Wait1 --> A47
    
    A48 --> A49[ArcA-P activated response regulator]
    A49 --> A50[ArcA-P dimerizes]
    A50 --> A51[ArcA-P binds Arc boxes in DNA]
    
    A51 --> A52[/ArcA-P represses aerobic genes/]
    A52 --> A53{Which aerobic genes?}
    A53 -->|cyoABCDE| A54[Cytochrome bo oxidase - high affinity]
    A53 -->|icdA| A55[Isocitrate dehydrogenase - TCA cycle]
    A53 -->|acnA| A56[Aconitase A - TCA cycle]
    A53 -->|gltA| A57[Citrate synthase - TCA cycle]
    A53 -->|sdhCDAB| A58[Succinate dehydrogenase overlap with FNR]
    
    A54 --> A59[/TCA cycle repressed/]
    A55 --> A59
    A56 --> A59
    A57 --> A59
    A58 --> A59
    
    A51 --> A60[ArcA-P activates anaerobic genes]
    A60 --> A61{Which anaerobic genes?}
    A61 -->|cydAB| A62[Cytochrome bd - microaerobic]
    A61 -->|focA| A63[Formate transport]
    A61 -->|pepT| A64[Peptidase for peptide utilization]
    
    A62 --> A65[Microaerobic respiration]
    A63 --> A66[Fermentation support]
    A64 --> A67[Alternative nitrogen sources]
    
    A25 --> A68{Electron acceptor hierarchy}
    A26 --> A68
    A27 --> A68
    
    A68 -->|Preference order| A69[1. Nitrate highest energy]
    A69 --> A70[2. DMSO intermediate]
    A70 --> A71[3. Fumarate lower energy]
    A71 --> A72[4. Fermentation lowest - no external acceptor]
    
    A28 --> A72
    
    A59 --> A73[Aerobic metabolism shut down]
    A34 --> A73
    
    A73 --> A74{FNR and ArcA coordination}
    A74 -->|Overlapping targets| A75[/Synergistic repression - e.g. sdh/]
    A74 -->|Distinct targets| A76[Complementary regulation]
    
    A75 --> A77[Complete aerobic-to-anaerobic transition]
    A76 --> A77
    
    A65 --> A77
    A66 --> A77
    A67 --> A77
    A25 --> A77
    A26 --> A77
    A27 --> A77
    A72 --> A77
    
    A77 --> End1([Anaerobic metabolism established])
    
    A3 --> A78[Aerobic respiration via cytochrome oxidases]
    A78 --> A79[TCA cycle produces NADH]
    A79 --> A80[Oxidative phosphorylation - high ATP yield]
    
    A80 --> End2([Aerobic metabolism])

name: Anaerobic Respiration Regulation (ArcAB and FNR)
notes: The FNR and ArcAB systems provide complementary regulation of the aerobic-anaerobic transition. FNR is a direct oxygen sensor (via Fe-S cluster), while ArcB senses oxygen indirectly (via quinone redox state). This dual sensing ensures robust response. The systems have overlapping but distinct regulons, with FNR primarily activating anaerobic genes and ArcA primarily repressing aerobic genes. The electron acceptor hierarchy (nitrate > DMSO > fumarate > fermentation) is determined by energetics. This system is highly conserved in facultative anaerobes and is critical for E. coli's ecological versatility.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive genetic and biochemical characterization of E. coli anaerobic regulation. All components (FNR, ArcA, ArcB), sensing mechanisms (Fe-S cluster oxygen sensitivity, quinone sensing), and target genes have been validated through decades of research. The model represents how bacteria optimize energy production under varying oxygen availability.

sources.title: Redox sensing by the ArcB sensor kinase of Escherichia coli: quinone as a direct redox signal
